common_brand_tagline: Switch on curiosity. Make the world a little more fun.
company_message_body: I believe the small spark of curiosity, the feeling of “I want to try that!” or “That looks fun!”, can become the force that moves our lives and the world forward. There is still so much value in the world waiting to be discovered. An app that makes everyday cooking a little more enjoyable. An exhibition that introduces you to ways of living you never knew before. What they all share is the ability to gently light someone else's curiosity. To make the world a little more interesting and a little more hopeful. CurioSwitch will keep creating more of those switches.
company_message_title: Switch on curiosity, and make the world a little more fun.
company_mission_body: The world starts moving with the quiet “What is this?” that lives inside us. We create room to discover wonder in the everyday things we take for granted. And we shape research and development born from curiosity into forms that can reach society.
company_mission_summary: Switch on curiosity and make the world a little more fun.
company_mission_title: Turn on curiosity. Make the world a bit more fun.
company_overview_address_value: Nishi-Shinjuku Mizuma Building 2F, 3-3-13 Nishi-Shinjuku, Shinjuku-ku, Tokyo 160-0023, Japan
company_overview_business_value: CurioSwitch is a creative studio that creates experiences at the intersection of ideas and technology, with research and development as its starting point. Our work focuses on R&D support, prototyping, and bringing products and services into society.
company_vision_body: Ideas are seeds for the future. Technology is the force that helps them grow into reality. Through research-born technology and creativity, we create moments that stay with people. CurioSwitch moves the world forward through ideas and technology.
company_vision_summary: Ideas and technology can create new value and new experiences for society.
company_vision_title: Create new value and experiences in society through ideas and technology.
footer_contact_description: If you have questions about our services or would like an estimate, feel free to reach out.
home_about_description: We are a creative team that brings excitement into everyday life through the power of technology and design. Together with research institutions in food, biotech, medicine, and biodiversity, we create products that truly serve humanity and the future of our environment.
home_feature_creative_lab_description: We create in-house services born from the intersection of technology and design. With our voice-interactive cooking service COOPii, we aim to support health from the dining table and turn everyday meal-making into a more enjoyable experience.
home_feature_design_exhibition_descripti: We create exhibition experiences that go beyond conventional formats through the power of design and technology. By combining visual beauty with digital technology, we deliver deeply immersive experiences that stay with visitors long after they leave.
home_feature_studio_description: Together with research institutions working in fields such as food, biotech, medicine, and biodiversity, we help bring advanced technology into society. From planning and design to development, we build products that turn research outcomes into services people continue to use.
service_engineering_ai_note: AI is no longer optional in modern product development. We integrate the latest AI capabilities into products, including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, voice interfaces, and image analysis, to upgrade the experience itself. We are especially strong in practical AI applications such as real-time voice interaction, recipe generation, and UI generation.
service_engineering_closing: We design UI and design systems with implementation in mind from the prototyping stage onward so that designers and engineers can build from a shared, explicit intent. By unifying design and technology in this way, we create a development process with less waste and a more polished user experience.
service_engineering_description: Turning ideas into real products and delivering them into users' hands
service_engineering_section_1_body: What matters in development is not simply building something, but implementing the right design for the goal. Based on the planning, strategy, and prototype work, we determine the most effective technical choices and implementation approach. Using a broad stack including React / Next.js, Go, Java, cloud infrastructure, and generative AI APIs, we shape products quickly and reliably.
service_engineering_section_1_title: Execution and judgment for building something that works, fast
service_engineering_section_2_body: Architecture is essential if a product is going to remain stable and keep evolving after launch. We design for performance, security, and maintainability, building a foundation that can handle future feature additions and user growth. Our goal is engineering that lasts, not something that merely works for now.
service_engineering_section_2_title: Architecture that balances quality and scalability
service_oss_body: At CurioSwitch, we actively contribute to open source projects. By returning the technology and know-how we have developed in-house to society and sharing them in forms that anyone can access, we aim to create a world where more people can turn ideas into reality. Libraries, design tools, and research outcomes developed internally are published on GitHub and nurtured as places of collaboration with creators and engineers outside the company. Supporting people who build is something we put into practice every day through open source.
service_oss_description: At CurioSwitch, we actively contribute to open source projects.
service_planning_closing: Planning and strategy are the foundation that shapes a project's future. We create the starting point that helps research take root in society through the power of ideas and technology.
service_planning_description: Designing the strategy that connects research and development to real-world implementation
service_planning_section_1_body: Vision, user value, competitive context, and feasibility all intersect in complex ways when shaping a plan. Research and development in particular can be difficult to express in a form that resonates with people. We carefully untangle those elements to identify what truly matters, then design the core concept that becomes the framework for the entire initiative.
service_planning_section_1_title: Expressing what matters most in a single phrase
service_planning_section_2_body: To bring research and development into society, it is necessary to design where, for whom, and in what way the technology will create value. Through market research, persona development, and customer journey analysis, we define how that value should be delivered and the strategy that leads there. Rather than relying on trends or surface-level tactics, we prioritize efforts that continue to matter over the long term.
service_planning_section_2_title: Bringing the value of research to society
service_prototyping_closing: Prototyping is not just about making a rough version of something. It is a learning process that gives form to the concept and user value clarified through planning and strategy while continually testing assumptions. By moving quickly through the cycle of planning, prototyping, validation, and improvement, products evolve with less hesitation and in a better direction.
service_prototyping_description: Pulling ideas out of your head and into the real world
service_prototyping_section_1_body: From wireframes and interaction studies to screen flows, mockups, and lightweight working demos, we build the right prototype for the goal in the shortest possible time. Ideas that were still vague in words or conversation become concrete all at once, making it much easier to decide whether to move forward and which direction to take.
service_prototyping_section_2_body: By using prototypes for user testing and interviews, we can quickly understand where people get stuck and where they find value. One of the greatest benefits of prototyping is that it prevents expensive rework later and delivers the greatest learning for the lowest cost.
service_uiux_closing: We design interfaces with component structure, responsive behavior, and technical constraints in mind so that the work holds up during implementation. Because the bridge between design and code is smoother, the development flow avoids unnecessary rework.
service_uiux_description: Balancing beauty with ease of use
service_uiux_section_1_body: We begin by carefully organizing the target audience, usage scenarios, challenges, and goals, then design the UX structure around user behavior. By building screen composition, information architecture, and navigation flows in a logical way, we create experiences people can use without hesitation.
service_uiux_section_2_body: We design UI elements such as color, spacing, typography, motion, and design systems to align with the brand's world and personality. By balancing a product's individuality with usability, we create experiences that feel both distinctive and comfortable to use.
service_user_testing_closing: Even before the code is complete, most issues can be uncovered through tests using Figma prototypes or simple demos. At CurioSwitch, we believe in validating before investing too deeply in implementation.
service_user_testing_description: Bringing real user voices back into the product, fast
service_user_testing_section_1_body: Rather than simply asking people to try the product, we test with scenarios based on what users are actually trying to do and why. This helps us visualize and analyze where they hesitate, what fails to come across, and where they feel real value.
service_user_testing_section_1_title: Designing tests around real usage scenarios
service_user_testing_section_2_body: Even when a UI looks polished, small sources of friction often appear the moment someone uses it. Unclear tap targets, duplicated navigation paths, confusing wording, and loading timing all have an outsized effect on the quality of the experience. User testing helps uncover these easy-to-miss issues early and makes it easier to prioritize improvements.
members_choko_career_1: Open source: Contributed gRPC support for Armeria and helped build high-performance decoders
members_choko_career_2: Amazon: Developed and launched the OpenTelemetry Java SDK and drove integration with AWS services
members_choko_career_3: LINE: Led infrastructure re-architecture and API development for LINE Shop
members_choko_career_4: Google: Worked on the Google Maps architecture refresh and YouTube caption features
members_choko_education_1: M.S. in Information Systems Engineering, Osaka University
members_choko_education_2: B.S. in Computer Science, University of California San Diego
members_choko_intro_1: Choko has extensive development experience at Google, Amazon, and LINE, and actively contributes to open source. Working end to end from infrastructure to frontend, Choko's strengths include API design and high-performance software development.
members_choko_intro_2: In recent years, Choko has also worked on GenAI-related development and technical support for startups, helping projects move from research and development to real-world deployment.
members_description: CurioSwitch is a creative team that turns ideas into reality through design and technology, creating new value for society.
members_kyon_career_1: Nippon Telegraph and Telephone Corporation: UI/UX Design
members_kyon_education_1: Keio University Graduate School of Media and Governance
members_kyon_education_2: Faculty of Environment and Information Studies, Keio University
members_kyon_intro_1: Kyon is a UI/UX designer and product manager who loves running and natto. After studying design thinking at Keio University Graduate School of Media and Governance, Kyon helped bring “Kawaii Search” to audiences across Japan.
members_kyon_intro_2: At NTT's research labs, Kyon worked on planning, experience design, and design implementation for interactive exhibitions such as Henshin Kabuki and Deformation Lamps. The work has been exhibited in Japan and internationally, including at CeBIT and Ars Electronica Festival in the United States, Germany, and Austria.
members_kyon_intro_3: After working at the XR startup MESON, Kyon joined ENJIN TOKYO in planning and creative roles, building value from both the product and experience sides.
news_description: Stay up to date with the latest from CurioSwitch. We share news about project launches, event exhibitions, service releases, and other updates on our work.
works_description: Explore a selection of projects created by CurioSwitch.
